initial_request_score.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: accrual of a longitudinal biofluid resource in patients, controls and healthy people at risk of prion diseases
potential to predict clinical onset in healthy people remains unclear... applied for preclinical biomarker discovery
ascertainment of rates of change as an even more sensitive predictor of disease onset

issues.claim: PrP-amyloid seeding activity, tau, and neurofilament-light (NfL)
issues.reason: Omits GFAP which is highlighted as a 'new proximity marker' and key finding in the abstract and conclusion, though tau and NfL are measured.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Include GFAP in target_name to reflect the key biomarker findings, e.g., 'PrP-amyloid seeding activity, GFAP, tau, and neurofilament-light (NfL)'.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: We studied 648 CSF and plasma samples, including 16 people who had samples taken when healthy but later developed inherited prion disease (IPD)
range from 9.9 prior to, and 7.4 years after onset

issues.reason: No citations provided in the extracted JSON for this step, reducing traceability score.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add relevant citations from the paper (e.g., NPMC study description in Introduction/Methods) to the references field.

evidence.quote: Second generation (IQ-CSF) RT-QuIC proved 100% sensitive and specific
Ha90 rPrP in pH7.4 and 300 mM NaCl at 55°C (IQ-CSF RT-QuIC)
Orru CD, Groveman BR, Foutz A, et al. Ring trial of 2nd generation RT-QuIC diagnostic tests for sporadic CJD. Ann Clin Transl Neurol. 2020

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: A bespoke HuPrP P102L RT-QuIC showed partial sensitivity for P102L disease

evidence.quote: No compatible RT-QuIC assay was discovered for classical 6-OPRI, A117V and D178N
